title: Simulate user-stopped
body:
### Feature request description

## description
i have been using Podman for more than two year,  recently i tried podman container auto-retart again, 
My requirement is for all containers to start automatically when the system boots, but containers that I stop manually will not start automatically at boot.
I am very frustrated because I did a lot of work but still couldn't succeed. Besides looking at new documents, I tried writing scripts to configure systemctl or use podman generate. The most disappointing thing is, I came across quadlet, but after I tried to understand it, it didn't work (podman version is 5.4.3) and was difficult to use.



### Suggest potential solution

## user-stop label and daemon command
I hope to add a user-stop status bit to check whether it was manually stopped by the user. The activation of this status can be done by adding a parameter to the stop command or by directly adding a new command, with the status name using 'user-stop' to prevent ambiguity and possible future conflicts. 
Once we have the user-stop status bit, we can use the daemon command to automatically create a system service. Its function is to check all existing containers, and if the policy is 'unless-stopped' and the status is not 'user-stopped', it will start that container. The daemon command uses the -user parameter to write and enable it for the current user.

### Have you considered any alternatives?


## Some thoughts
I think the need for automatic startup on boot is quite necessary. Because this feature is not supported, several of my servers and NAS are still using Docker, even though I have been using Podman on my personal computer for many years without any other problems. 
In addition, I think this also affects the support for Podman on other server panels. Containers that you neither want to start nor want to delete will start again when the system restarts. Beginners will not consider the drawbacks of the daemon and will care more about convenience.
Believe me, most people do not pay attention to and often start and stop the services they commonly use (so it's necessary to enable auto-start in batches), but they will indeed stop a service they do not want to start casually.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body: This has already been implemented in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/28236 which should have laned in 5.8.2 already
